reason_for_decision: The article passes because it reframes perimeter protection around zone ownership, timing, and operator workflow instead of treating the fence as the whole design problem.
strengths: The article is strong on breaking the site into boundary, gate, yard, roofline, and airspace roles instead of flattening everything into one fence requirement.
It gives real decision value by connecting detection, verification, delay, communications, and evidence retention into one perimeter architecture.
The discussion of operator queues and zone ownership makes the piece feel operationally grounded rather than abstract.
summary_assessment: This is a strong applied knowledge-base article. It gives planners a credible framework for data center perimeter design and ties physical coverage to response timing and workflow discipline.
